projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: [front_yard] The front yard camera is currently offline due to a connectivity error.

The household experienced a significant, coherent activity spike starting around 15:53, indicating the arrival of a visitor or the family's return, coupled with Steve unpacking a large delivery. The high activity level, combined with the multiple technical failures (front yard camera, dog run camera), suggests that the full context of the visit or delivery is currently obscured and warrants manual review of the video logs from the 15:50-16:15 window.
Unusually high activity at front_door this hour: 4 observations vs typical 1 for weekend 16:00
Unusually high activity at living_room this hour: 4 observations vs typical 1 for weekend 16:00
[front_yard] The front yard camera is experiencing intermittent connection failures, which may lead to gaps in security monitoring.

The sequence of the doorbell ring (15:08), escalating unclassified audio, and culminating in urgent dog barking (15:13) strongly suggests a recent external stimulus or visitor, despite the cameras showing the yard is currently empty. This pattern indicates activity that warrants checking the perimeter or confirming the nature of the disturbance.
[dog_run] The dog_run camera is reporting a technical error: 'No frames have been received, check error logs'.

There is a significant conflict between the presence system (12 mobile devices reported home at 14:13) and the visual sensors, which consistently report the entire house and yard as empty and quiet. Furthermore, the doorbell rang twice (14:10 and 14:13), suggesting recent activity or a visitor, despite the cameras showing no one. This indicates either an unobserved event or a potential error in the occupancy detection.
[dog_run] An error icon is visible on the camera feed, indicating a potential technical issue.
[front_yard] The front yard camera is experiencing a persistent outage, making it impossible to monitor the front yard and street.
